description: Shared source-native noise policy. Global labels define common noise semantics; source rules map native sections, rank sections, URL patterns, and text exceptions to keep/drop/low_confidence.

sports.description: Sports match, athlete, league, and fan-coverage items that are usually outside the investor daily-news scope.

entertainment.description: Celebrity, music, film, culture, and soft entertainment items unless a source-specific keep override identifies a business angle.

lifestyle.description: Lifestyle, travel, food, book, and service content with low investor relevance.

photo_gallery.description: Photo gallery pages that usually duplicate or summarize events without adding a primary article.

video_only.description: Video-only items are retained only if another rule gives them enough relevance.

native_section_missing.description: The source-native section could not be extracted, so category assignment should be treated as less reliable.

ap.notes: AP homepage ranking is useful, but native sports and entertainment sections often enter the homepage Top list. Drop them unless a business, technology, policy, or market override is present.

bbc.notes: BBC adapter only enters News, Business, and Technology landing pages. This source-native noise policy is a final safety net for off-scope BBC URLs or sections; video/audio are retained by the adapter only when text summary or transcript-like text is available.

tiktok-profile-signals.notes: Profile-level TikTok snapshots are discovery diagnostics, not stable news items. Keep them out of the main daily-news candidate pool unless a future video-level adapter adds reliable titles, timestamps, and source context.

github-trending.notes: GitHub Trending is rendered in the standalone technical-dynamics section and should not also compete with general news clusters.

github-issues-trends.notes: GitHub issue heat is a technical signal surface. It is rendered in technical dynamics and excluded from the general news ranking pool.
